justification: Meets most pass criteria excellently — state machine directionality is thoroughly modeled (In Transit → Pending forbidden, Delivered terminal, even flags Pending → Delivered skip), 15+ acceptance criteria in Given/When/Then format, testable assertions are concrete and developer-actionable, confidence at 82 with extensive assumptions listed. However, the weight bracket pricing rule is classified as invariant: true (twice — rules 7 and 8), which directly violates a pass requirement. The rubric explicitly requires weight bracket marked invariant: false because it is a soft pricing/business rule, not a state invariant. This is also listed as a primary failure mode. The agent conflates 'derived and deterministic' with 'domain invariant', missing the distinction that pricing tier classification can change without breaking domain integrity (e.g., business could add a fourth tier without violating any shipment lifecycle constraint).

observations.text: State machine modeling is exceptional — captures forward-only directionality, explicitly forbids In Transit → Pending, marks Delivered as terminal, and even identifies Pending → Delivered skip as forbidden (going beyond the minimum)
Identified 8 rules total, exceeding the minimum 5, with granular separation (e.g., weight > 0 and weight ≤ 1000 as separate rules)
15 acceptance criteria in proper Given/When/Then format with boundary testing at 10 kg, 10.01 kg, 50 kg, 50.01 kg, and 1000 kg
Edge cases section is strong — identifies concurrent state transitions, weight mutability post-creation, identical addresses, and minimum weight precision
Confidence level of 82 is well-calibrated with 6 low-confidence areas and 6 explicit assumptions — demonstrates appropriate epistemic humility
Bounded context correctly identified as Shipment Management, with note that pricing calculation likely belongs to a separate Billing/Pricing context — yet still misclassifies the bracket rule
Open questions for PM are practical and demonstrate domain thinking (cancellation flow, boundary ambiguity, address validation depth)

Weight bracket classification marked invariant: true — this is the primary failure mode called out in the rubric. It is a soft pricing rule, not a domain invariant. The agent even notes pricing belongs to a separate bounded context but contradicts this by marking the bracket as invariant
Weight bracket boundary inclusivity rule (rule 8) is also marked invariant: true — doubling down on the misclassification by treating bracket boundary details as domain invariants
Business impact section, while thorough, was not requested by the rubric and adds bulk without addressing the invariant classification gap
